source_jobs.title: Operations Associate (Checkatrade Express)
source_jobs.description:
As our next Operations Associate (Checkatrade Express), you’ll sit at the centre of how one of our fastest-growing products evolves - helping shape the experience for both customers and trades, and driving the next phase of growth.

You’ll be working on Checkatrade Express, a newer, high-growth part of the business focused on making it faster and easier for customers to book everyday home services. It’s early, it’s scaling quickly, and there’s a huge opportunity to build something meaningful from the ground up.

Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Owning key areas of supply health - making sure we have the right trades, in the right places, at the right time to meet growing demand

 - Building frameworks and processes from scratch to support trade acquisition and quality as we scale

 - Working across product, data and operations to turn ideas into live features and improvements on the platform

 - Getting stuck into whatever problems need solving - whether that’s launching new services, improving supply, or fixing something that isn’t working

 - Collaborating closely with product managers, engineers and operators to push projects over the line

 - Using data to spot patterns, identify opportunities and help the team focus on what matters most

 - Helping shape how Checkatrade Express grows - contributing to strategy, not just execution

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Strong analytical thinking and comfort working with data (Excel essential, BI tools a bonus)

 - A proactive mindset - you ask questions, spot opportunities and take ownership

 - Clear communication skills and the ability to work across teams

 - Comfort operating in a fast-paced, changing environment where priorities can shift quickly

 - A genuine curiosity for how things work and how they can be improved

 - Early experience (or strong interest) in consulting, tech, startups or similar high-performance environments

 - An interest in using AI and automation to work smarter and scale your output

 - Ambition, drive and a willingness to get stuck in and learn quickly



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus or commission (role dependent)

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!


How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st Stage interview with the hiring manager focused on your experience

 - 2nd Stage interview with colleagues you will work alongside

 - Final interview which includes a case study presentation to 2-3 colleagues including the hiring manager

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

About us

We’re Checkatrade – the UK’s leading platform for finding a tradesperson. With more than 50,000 trades listed and over 6 million reviews, we help homeowners get jobs done right and help tradespeople grow their business.

We’re growing fast, with big plans to become the go-to brand for home improvements. If you care about great work, take pride in the details, and want to make an impact, you’ll fit right in.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Executive
source_jobs.description:
As our next Customer Success Executive, you’ll help us grow that impact. By connecting with trades and showing the value of joining Checkatrade, you’ll help build stronger businesses and stronger homes across the UK.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Engaging potential customers and converting leads into Checkatrade members.

 - Using solution-based selling to show how we support trade businesses to grow.

 - Handling objections with confidence and closing with clarity.

 - Keeping Salesforce data tidy, accurate and up to date.

 - Working with other teams to share insight, learn fast, and keep smashing targets.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Proven experience in a sales role, with a strong track record of hitting targets.

 - A confident communicator who’s not afraid to pick up the phone and make things happen.

 - Resilience, drive, and a hunger to exceed expectations.

 - Solid objection-handling and negotiation skills.

 - A proactive, self-motivated mindset and a knack for spotting opportunities.

 - Experience using Salesforce or a similar CRM to manage your pipeline.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Health, well-being and learning support

If this sounds like the place you’ll thrive, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every single box.

source_jobs.title: Sales Associate
source_jobs.description:
As our next Sales Associate, you’ll be on the front line of our growth. You’ll engage with trade businesses who are already interested in joining Checkatrade, build rapport fast, and turn conversations into long-term partnerships. This isn’t cold calling - it’s about converting warm leads into real impact.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Responding quickly to inbound leads by phone and email. No delays, no missed opportunities.

 - Understanding what customers need and showing how Checkatrade can help their business grow.

 - Explaining the value of Checkatrade clearly and handling objections with confidence.

 - Managing the full sales cycle, from first conversation through to closing the deal.

 - Keeping your activity and pipeline up to date in Salesforce.

 - Sharing insights from customer conversations to help spot trends, opportunities and competitor activity.

 - Working with internal teams to make sure new members get off to a smooth start.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience in inbound sales, direct sales or account management.

 - A track record of consistently hitting sales targets.

 - Resilience and determination in a fast-moving sales environment.

 - Confidence in handling objections and moving deals forward.

 - Strong written and verbal communication that builds trust quickly.

 - A proactive, curious mindset and interest in how markets move.

 - Experience using Salesforce or a similar CRM tool.
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + uncapped commission

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Head of Sales SME and Associate Manager, Inbound

 - 2nd stage interview with our Head of Sales SME and our Sales Operations Manager

 - 3rd stage interview with our VP of Sales



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
As our next Account Executive, you’ll help grow Checkatrade by bringing more trades onto the platform across different regions and categories.

This is a new business role focused on finding high-potential trade businesses and guiding them from the first conversation through to closing the deal. You’ll sit within our Strategic Growth team, working at the heart of our Go-To-Market function and playing a key role in expanding our trade network.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Winning new trade businesses. You’ll identify high-potential small and medium trades, build compelling proposals, negotiate terms, and hand them over smoothly to our onboarding team.

 - Spotting opportunities in the market. Using data and insights, you’ll understand where Checkatrade is strong, where we can grow, and where new opportunities exist.

 - Working across teams. You’ll partner closely with Account Management, Vetting, Product and Operations to identify regions and categories that can benefit most from Checkatrade.

 - Becoming a product expert. You’ll learn our product suite inside out so you can build strong business cases tailored to each prospect.

 - Owning the outbound sales cycle. From prospecting and qualification through to proposals, negotiation and closing deals.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Solid experience in commercial or new business sales, ideally in similar markets, with a strong focus on outbound acquisition.

 - A proven track record of identifying new clients, building tailored proposals, and hitting revenue targets.

 - The ability to build strong relationships with tradespeople and work closely with internal teams.

 - Confidence managing multiple priorities and working across teams to improve how things get done.

 - Experience building customer relationships, whether that’s through digital outreach or face-to-face conversations.

 - Familiarity with CRM systems (Salesforce), outbound tools, and an understanding of AI-powered optimisation tools.

 - Tenacity, motivation and resilience. This is a fast-moving, test-and-learn environment where persistence matters.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + commission

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Head of Commercial & Strategic Growth

 - 2nd Stage interview with our Category Principal and Senior Manager, Commercial Strategy & Sales



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Legal Counsel, Privacy & AI Governance
source_jobs.description:
As our next Legal Counsel, Privacy & AI Governance, you’ll be the go-to expert on some of the most genuinely interesting legal questions Checkatrade is working through right now. As our platform scales new products, new AI capabilities, new partnerships, you’ll sit at the intersection of data protection, AI governance, and cyber regulation, giving the business advice that’s useful and not just defensible. You’ll sit within a collaborative legal team that’s nominated for In-House Team of the Year and you’ll have real space to build: smarter tools, better processes, and a legal function that scales with the business.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Advising product, commercial, and operational teams on data protection and privacy law, giving practical, risk-calibrated guidance that keeps delivery moving and keeps the business on the right side of the law.

 - Drafting and negotiating data protection agreements, including DPAs, SCCs, data sharing arrangements, and privacy provisions across commercial contracts.

 - Leading DPIAs and AI risk assessments from day one, working hand-in-hand with product and engineering to make sure new features and third-party integrations are built right from the outset.

 - Advising on AI governance and regulatory obligations, including lawful basis, training data governance, automated decision-making and profiling under UK GDPR, and translating the evolving UK AI regulatory landscape into clear, actionable guidance.

 - Owning the legal side of cyber and information security obligations, including NIS2 and the UK Cyber Security and Resilience Bill, and leading legal risk assessment and regulatory notification when incidents occur.

 - Spotting opportunities to make legal work better, including identifying and building legal AI solutions, testing new tools, and rolling out workflow improvements that help the whole team do more with less.

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - A strong track record of privacy and data protection legal advice, ideally in-house or advising digital, tech, or consumer-facing businesses, with the confidence to apply UK GDPR, DPA 2018, and PECR to novel fact patterns, not just textbook scenarios.

 - Genuine enthusiasm for legal technology and AI tools, with hands-on experience experimenting with or deploying solutions that make legal teams work smarter.

 - Experience drafting and negotiating data protection contracts, including DPAs and cross-border transfer mechanisms, alongside working knowledge of AI governance and cyber regulatory obligations.

 - The ability to engage credibly with product, engineering, and commercial stakeholders, translating legal risk into clear guidance that enables delivery rather than blocking it.


We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!


How we’ll get to know each other: e.g:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with Hiring Manager

 - 2nd Stage interview Case study

 - Final stage interview with Senior Leadership

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
As we continue to expand our platform and enhance our offerings, we are seeking a visionary Senior Product Manager to lead the development of Checkatrade’s new advertising products. This role offers a unique opportunity to drive innovation, shape the future of advertising within our ecosystem, and unlock new growth opportunities for our company.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Designing new advertising products as well as upgrading the ones already in place. You’ll take what works today, make it stronger, and develop entirely new ways for trades to reach customers.

 - Using real insight to shape what we build next. Turning user behaviour, market trends and performance data into bold product decisions that actually move metrics.

 - Working closely with engineers, designers, data scientists and commercial teams. Getting into the details, shipping quickly and solving problems together - not lobbing tickets over the fence.

 - Experimenting like it’s a sport. A/B tests, pricing trials, performance tuning, live-data tweaks - whatever it takes to figure out what “great” should look like.

 - Driving the launch of an entirely new growth stream for Checkatrade. Your ideas won’t sit in a backlog. They’ll help shape the next generation of how advertisers connect with customers.
   

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience in product management is a must-have. Consulting, ad tech or digital marketing is a bonus. You understand how digital experiences work, and you’re comfortable shaping something new.

 - A strategic, product-thinking mindset. You can zoom out to see the opportunity, then translate it into clear priorities, trade-offs and next steps.

 - Strong analytical instincts and confidence with experiments. Whether it’s digging into data, interpreting results, or using insights to drive decisions, you’re comfortable proving (or disproving) ideas.

 - A collaborative, engineering-friendly approach. You enjoy working closely with designers, data teams, engineers and commercial partners to get things built.

 - Problem-solving clarity. You can simplify complexity, cut through noise, and turn messy problems into actionable product decisions.

 - Clear communication and storytelling. You can articulate the “why”, bring people with you, and make complex ideas digestible for any audience.

 - Bias for action and ownership. You’re comfortable moving fast, making smart calls in ambiguous situations and taking ideas from concept to launch.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners.

 - 1st stage interview with our Head of Product and Operations (Media).

 - 2nd stage interview with our VP of Engineering and Principal Product Manager.

 - Final stage includes a case study presentation with our VP of Bookings, Directories & Media and our Head of Product & Operations (Media).



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Strategy and Operations Associate (Bookings)
source_jobs.description:
As our next Strategy and Operations Associate (Bookings Team), you’ll play a key role in helping us build the UK’s go-to home improvement marketplace.


You’ll be part of the Bookings team which is a team focused on transforming Checkatrade from a lead generation platform into a true end-to-end bookings and transactions marketplace, where customers can seamlessly book, manage and pay for services directly through Checkatrade.

We’ve built and shipped multiple products, including Checkatrade Express and Checkatrade for Business which are early-stage products that are already powering real bookings and helping us move towards this vision.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Help shape the future of home services as part of a small, ambitious and entrepreneurial team.

 - Tackle a wide range of challenges in a fast-moving team where priorities evolve quickly.

 - Working across strategy, product and data to test ideas, launch new features and solve meaningful problems.

 - Partnering closely with product managers, engineers and operators to turn ideas into live products that make a real impact.

 - Driving operational excellence. You’ll identify bottlenecks, improve processes and build scalable workflows that improve how our products perform day-to-day.
   

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Background in consulting, tech, or startups is helpful but not essential.

 - Strong critical thinking and a genuine interest in solving complex problems.

 - Comfortable working with data. Excel/Google Sheets is required, and experience with BI tools is a plus.

 - An AI-curious mindset. You’re interested in using automation and AI tools to work faster and have more impact.

 - Clear communication skills and the ability to adapt in a fast-moving environment where priorities can shift.
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support (including access to leadership programmes and mentorship)

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!
   

How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our team leads

 - 2nd Stage interview includes a case study interview with key stakeholders

 - Final stage interview includes a case study interview
   

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box.

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
As our next Director of Engineering, you’ll be the architect of our delivery engine. This isn't about sitting in the code all day; it's about shaping how engineering actually happens in an AI-first world. You’ll lead the systems, the people, and the workflows that allow us to ship world-class software at a pace that leaves the competition behind.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Defining the AI-enabled SDLC: Moving beyond simple "copilots" to integrate agentic workflows, automated reviews, and AI-driven incident response into our daily DNA.

 - Orchestrating the machine: Evolving our "Golden Paths," developer platforms, and CI/CD patterns so that "how work moves" from idea to production is a competitive advantage.

 - Leading through leaders: Mentoring 3–5 Engineering Managers to build high-accountability teams that ship frequently, with fewer surprises and higher quality.

 - Setting the technical agenda: Making the high-stakes calls on architecture, reliability, and security across a significant slice of our estate.

 - Driving high-intensity execution: Removing obstacles with pace and ensuring our ambitious roadmap becomes a reality through clarity and technical judgment.
   

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Deep technical credibility: You can hold your own with staff engineers on distributed systems, security risks, and performance bottlenecks.

 - An AI-forward mindset: You have a strong POV on how agentic patterns and human-in-the-loop design transform engineering throughput.

 - Systems thinking: You focus on the system of work standardising where it helps and granting autonomy where it matters.

 - Proven leadership at scale: You’ve successfully led multiple teams through managers and know how to build a strong leadership bench.

 - A hunger for pace: You thrive in high-intensity environments where expectations are high, and the "typical" corporate speed isn't enough.
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Chief Technology Officer

 - 2nd Stage interview with our VP, R&D Operations

 - 3rd Stage interview with our Director of Engineering

Please note that some of the above stakeholders may change due to availability



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Strategic Account Specialist
source_jobs.description:
As our next Strategic Account Specialist, you’ll play a key role in helping us build the UK’s go-to home improvement marketplace. From driving revenue through smarter product adoption to helping tradespeople grow on the platform, your work will help millions of homeowners find the right tradesperson for the job, fast, fair, and without the faff.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Selling - run a proactive outbound sales motion to upsell revenue through the adoption and optimisation of Checkatrade’s diverse suite of products

 - Strategic planning and execution - develop robust monthly action plans for your portfolio with deep knowledge of your accounts’ performance goals/challenges

 - Inbound issue resolution and mitigation - run internal investigations and proactively identify and problem solve to remove blockers to trade growth on the platform

 - Relationship and credibility building - build high-trust, credible relationships through frequent touch points and strategic support for accounts
   

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - A hybrid hunter/farmer mindset, motivated by hitting short-term targets while building long-term value

 - Resilience. You stay focused and keep moving, even when things don’t go to plan

 - A strong sense of ownership over your portfolio, performance, and commercial outcomes

 - A collaborative, people-first approach. You build relationships that make people want to work with you

 - Commercial experience managing a book of revenue, with a track record of driving results

 - Confidence using CRM and AI tools, or the curiosity to pick them up quickly

 - Adaptability, curiosity, and a willingness to learn fast
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus or commission (role dependent)

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more
   

How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with the Head of Account Management

 - 2nd stage interviews with Senior Stakeholders

 - Final stage including a case study presentation
   

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box.

source_jobs.title: Credit Control Agent
source_jobs.description:
As our next Credit Control Agent, you’ll help keep things running smoothly behind the scenes. This isn’t just about chasing payments. It’s about helping our trade members stay financially healthy, supported, and active on the platform, so they can keep growing their business, and we can keep building ours.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Making and receiving calls from existing trade members to resolve payment issues.

 - Setting up direct debits, processing card payments, and agreeing on realistic payment terms.

 - Listening carefully, solving problems, and removing barriers to payment, with empathy and a commercial mindset.

 - Keeping accurate records across systems like Zuora and Salesforce.

 - Supporting team goals while delivering top-class service that builds trust and gets results.

 - A positive, proactive attitude and a genuine desire to help people.

 - Strong admin skills and great attention to detail when recording data.

 - Flexibility to adapt to process changes and a cool head when things get busy.

 - The ability to work well in a team, with empathy, resilience, and a people-first approach.

 - Experience in a credit control, customer service or billing environment is a bonus but not essential. Full training is provided.

source_jobs.title: Strategy and Operations - Revenue
source_jobs.description:
As our next Strategy and Operations Lead within our Sales Revenue team, you will own the operational infrastructure that powers Checkatrade's trade acquisition and activation engine. This isn't a traditional Sales Ops role — Sales Ops at Checkatrade sits at the intersection of Sales, Marketing, Account Management, Finance, and Product, ensuring every team involved in generating revenue is aligned, data-informed, and operationally excellent.



You'll shape how we attract, qualify, convert, onboard, and upgrade tradespeople across our membership tiers and new products. SalesOps at a two-sided marketplace is different — you'll be optimising the supply side, balancing acquisition volume with quality, and supporting a sales team that sells to tradespeople who are simultaneously our product and our customers.



This is a hands-on, high-impact role with a direct line of sight to revenue outcomes and a seat at the table in commercial planning.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Technology & Systems Strategy: Support with the revenue tech stack strategy (Salesforce, Gong, Clay and supporting tools), ensuring it scales with Checkatrade's growth and supports a unified view of the trader journey from lead to activated member

 - Revenue Intelligence & Forecasting: Build and maintain a revenue analytics framework tracking pipeline health, conversion rates, revenue. Also own the sales forecasting model — combining pipeline coverage, deal velocity, win rates, and seasonal patterns to deliver reliable revenue predictions

 - Process Architecture & GTM Operations: Map, document, and continuously improve end-to-end processes from lead assignment and qualification through to close, onboarding, and first campaign activation. Also, identify bottlenecks in the sales workflow and design scalable solutions

 - Cross-Functional Alignment: Act as the connective tissue between Sales, Marketing, Account Management, Finance and Product — ensuring teams operate from shared definitions, data and goals



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Breadth of experience in RevOps/SalesOps, Consulting or a similar strategic operational role

 - Strong Salesforce proficiency — comfortable building reports, dashboards, and flows, with a good understanding of declarative capabilities and data architecture

 - SQL or equivalent data querying skills — able to self-serve data beyond pre-built reports

 - An analytical mindset with proven ability to translate data into actionable, commercial recommendations

 - Proficiency with BI tools (e.g., Tableau) for defining metrics frameworks and building self-serve reporting

 - Experience designing and documenting sales or revenue processes end-to-end



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Sales Operations Manager

 - 2nd stage interview will be two speed rounds with stakeholders within Sales

 - Final interview will be a case study with the Sales Operations Manager and VP of Sales



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Trust & Safety Associate
source_jobs.description:
As our next Trust & Safety Associate, you’ll play a key role in helping us build the UK’s go-to home improvement marketplace. From vetting and verifying every tradesperson who joins our platform to maintaining the trust and quality standards our customers rely on, your work will help ensure homeowners can connect with skilled, reliable professionals with confidence. By safeguarding the integrity of our marketplace , your work will help millions of homeowners find the right tradesperson for the job - fast, fair, and without the faff.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Vetting and verifying tradespeople to ensure only high-quality, trustworthy professionals join the platform.

 - Delivering a fair, efficient, and customer-focused review process that drives confidence for homeowners and trades alike.

 - Spotting ways to improve our Trust & Safety processes and putting ideas into action.

 - Collaborating with Customer Support, Commercial, Product, and Operations teams to solve problems and improve outcomes.

 - Investigating risks, identifying patterns, and helping protect the integrity of the marketplace.

 - Making sure every interaction leaves customers and tradespeople feeling confident in Checkatrade.

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience in Trust & Safety, customer operations, fraud prevention, quality assurance, compliance, or a similar field or a genuine eagerness to learn.

 - A collaborative, roll-up-your-sleeves attitude and a willingness to get stuck in.

 - Strong communication skills, good judgement, and a natural curiosity to investigate and solve problems.

 - Confidence working with systems, internal tools, and digital platforms, or the ability to pick them up quickly.

 - Great attention to detail and the ability to make fair, consistent decisions.

 - A passion for helping create a trusted marketplace where customers and tradespeople can thrive.

source_jobs.title: Commercial Growth Associate Manager
source_jobs.description:
As Commercial Growth Associate Manager, you'll have genuine ownership over some of the most commercially significant work in the business. You'll shape how we price, how we grow our member base, and how we use data, experimentation, and AI to build a smarter, more scalable commercial operation. It's a broad remit with real autonomy - the kind of role where the quality of your thinking directly determines the outcome.



This is a doing role. You'll move fast, work across multiple programmes at once, and drive delivery with others — not just define the strategy.

 



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Lead commercial programmes end-to-end: identify the opportunity, build the business case, define the strategy, and drive cross-functional teams to deliver — from first principles to impact.

 - Own our member engagement and growth strategy: identify where we can deepen value for our existing trade members, develop the commercial rationale, and lead initiatives across Product, CRM, and Sales to bring those programmes to life.

 - Drive subscription performance: analyse the health of our member lifecycle, identify where value is being lost, and lead the programmes that improve efficiency — from churn reduction to smarter commercial interventions.

 - Shape our pricing strategy: build the tracking, benchmarking, and alerting systems that keep our pricing sharp, and lead the strategic thinking behind how we evolve our model over time.

 - Build the case for intelligent operations: identify where AI and automation can give us commercial leverage, lead the business case, and drive delivery of the tools and systems that make this team faster and smarter.




It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Autonomous problem solver: you work independently, make complex decisions confidently, and bring structure to ambiguous challenges without needing a playbook.

 - Commercial and marketplace instinct: you understand how pricing, retention, and member value interact — and you use that to spot opportunities others miss.

 - Strong analytical foundation: fluent in SQL and advanced spreadsheets, you turn messy datasets into clear recommendations that drive decisions, not just decks.

 - Persuasive communicator: you lead cross-functional conversations with confidence and know how to build the case that gets stakeholders bought in and moving.

 - Relevant experience: you've worked in strategy, operations, or growth roles — ideally within a consulting firm or a fast-paced consumer, marketplace, or digital platform business.
   
    

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Marketplace Operations Manager

 - 2nd stage interview will be a case study with our VP of Marketplace and stakeholders

 - 3rd stage interview will be with our Marketplace Operations Manager and VP of Marketplace

If you require any reasonable adjustments during our interview process, please discuss them with your Talent Acquisition Partner.



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Strategy and Operations Manager (Product)
source_jobs.description:
As we continue to expand our platform and evolve how we drive growth, we’re looking for a Strategy & Operations Manager (Product) to lead the development and scaling of Checkatrade’s advertising proposition.

This role sits right at the intersection of product, strategy and commercial execution - giving you a unique opportunity to shape how advertising works within our ecosystem and unlock entirely new revenue streams.

Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Shape and grow our advertising offering. Spot what’s working, uncover new opportunities, and help define where we go next.

 - Turn ideas into real products and features. You’ll work closely with product, engineering, and commercial teams to bring them to life.

 - Use data to guide decisions. From performance and user behaviour to market trends, you’ll help decide where we invest next.

 - Connect teams across the business. You’ll bring engineers, designers, sales and commercial teams together to keep things moving.

 - Test, learn, improve, repeat. Whether it’s pricing, positioning or new formats, you’ll experiment constantly and move quickly.

 - Build a new growth area for Checkatrade. This is more than optimisation. You’ll help shape a key part of our future business.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience in product, strategy, operations, or a similar role. Experience in consulting, ad tech or marketplaces is a plus.

 - Strategic thinking with a practical streak. You can spot opportunities and turn them into clear next steps.

 - Confidence with data. You’re comfortable analysing performance, running experiments, and using insights to guide decisions.

 - Collaborative by nature. You work well with product, engineering and commercial teams to get things moving.

 - Strong problem-solving skills. You can bring structure to messy challenges and make complex things feel simple.

 - Clear communicator. You can explain your thinking, influence decisions, and bring others with you.

 - Proactive and hands-on. You’re comfortable with ambiguity and happy making decisions at pace.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus or commission (role dependent)

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!

source_jobs.title: Head of Mid Market Sales
source_jobs.description:
As a Head of Mid-Market Sales, you will build and lead our Mid-Market function at Checkatrade.



This is a 0→1→100 leadership role. You’ll take ownership of a largely undefined space and turn it into a scalable, high-performing revenue engine - defining the motion, closing initial deals, and building a team that can deliver consistently beyond you.



As we evolve from a transactional model to a more structured, value-led and SaaS-oriented approach, you’ll play a critical role in shaping how we win in the Mid-Market: establishing the sales playbook, embedding pipeline discipline, and developing a team of high-performing Account Executives.



You’ll work closely with Product, Marketing, CSM, and Revenue Operations to ensure we’re not just closing deals, but building a motion that drives activation, retention, and long-term value.



This is a high-impact, high-ownership leadership role - where you’ll be expected to lead from the front, build from the ground up, and scale through others.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Revenue & Pipeline: You will own the Mid-Market revenue target across both leads and non-leads segments, while building and maintaining 3–5× pipeline coverage with clear visibility and control. You will also run end-to-end sales cycles: discovery → demo → pilot → commercial close

 - Playbook & Go-to-Market: You will define and document the repeatable Mid-Market sales motion while testing and iterating on outbound sequences, messaging, and objection handling

 - Team Building: You will hire, onboard, and coach two Account Executives and define performance standards, ramp plans, and quota frameworks. You will also act as a player-coach — carrying a personal number while building the team and set the tone for a high-accountability, high-performance culture

 - Insight & Reporting: You will own pipeline reporting and forecasting, presenting weekly updates to leadership (TradeAI Steering Committee). You will also feed real-time market and customer insights back into Product, Marketing, and leadership



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Proven SaaS sales leadership experience with some experience managing Mid-Market or SMB+ teams (£10k–£60k ACV)

 - Track record of building and scaling teams — hiring, onboarding, and developing Account Executives in a pre-scale or growth environment

 - Consistent delivery through others — not just personal quota attainment, but driving team performance against target with clear pipeline discipline

 - Experience operating in 0→1 or 1→10 environments — comfortable creating structure, not just following it

 - Strong coaching capability — able to diagnose performance gaps, run effective deal reviews, and improve win rates across the team

 - Pipeline and forecasting rigour — confident running weekly pipeline reviews and delivering accurate, data-backed forecasts through the team

 - Structured, value-led selling mindset — able to embed a repeatable sales methodology across reps



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + commission (role dependent)

 - Employee Share Program

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with the VP of Sales

 - 2nd stage interview and case study with Cross Functional Senior Stakeholders

 - Final stage interview with the COO



If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
As our next Principal Product Manager, Consumer Product, you'll take full ownership of the homeowner experience across web and app, one of the most important levers we have for building the UK's most trusted home improvement marketplace. This is a role where your decisions will be felt by millions of homeowners every year: how they search, how they choose, how much they trust what they find. You'll set the vision, build the systems and raise the bar on quality, all while keeping the team moving at real pace.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Defining an ambitious, long-term vision for the Checkatrade consumer experience then turning it into a focused, deliverable roadmap that the team can actually execute against.

 - Setting and relentlessly defending the standard for what a best-in-class homeowner experience looks like, and holding the team accountable to it not just in theory, but in every shipped feature.

 - Partnering hand-in-hand with Engineering to break down the technical and organisational blockers that slow delivery, owning the outcome, not just the spec.

 - Owning the full consumer funnel, discoverability, conversion, trust, satisfaction and retention and using data, experimentation and insight to move the needle on all of it.

 - Building and raising the standards of a high-performing, cross-functional product team with clear ownership, a bias for action and an uncompromising eye for quality.

 - Acting as a visible custodian of quality across the organisation, aligning the roadmap to commercial outcomes and championing the consumer at every level of the business.

It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Extensive experience in senior product roles, with full end-to-end ownership of a consumer-facing product at scale and a track record of experiences that real people love and that move conversion, engagement and retention.

 - Boldness and vision: the ability to articulate a clear, ambitious view of what the product should become (not just an incremental improvement plan) and bring people with you on that journey.

 - Execution edge: you actually ship. You drive delivery with Engineering, remove blockers, hold pace and don't let quality slip while doing it.

 - Deep analytical rigour and commercial fluency you use experimentation, funnel analysis and consumer insight to drive decisions, and you understand how product choices connect to revenue and long-term consumer lifetime value.


We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!
   
   
   How we’ll get to know each other:

source_jobs.title: Trade Growth Lead (Maternity Cover)
source_jobs.description:
As our next Trade Growth Lead (Maternity Cover), you’ll own the growth journey from first touch through to first monetisation. Sitting within our Trade Experience product team, you’ll work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Design, Performance Marketing, CRM, and Commercial teams to help shape the future of how trades join and grow with Checkatrade. This is a high-impact role at the centre of one of the company’s most important strategic growth bets.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Own the Trade Growth Funnel — driving the full journey from sign-up to monetisation, increasing both volume and quality of self-serve trade sign-ups, and owning the metrics that matter.

 - Lead a high-tempo experimentation programme — running structured A/B, multivariate, and holdout tests across acquisition journeys, onboarding flows, freemium activation surfaces, and in-product conversion experiences.

 - Bring marketing instinct into product — translating positioning and messaging into product experiences, improving landing pages, sign-up flows, nudges, and lifecycle touchpoints, and working hand-in-hand with Performance Marketing and CRM to optimise end-to-end conversion.

 - Be the analytical centre of gravity — owning and evolving the funnel model that tracks every stage from impression to first paid action, so the team always knows where to focus next.

 - Work in an AI-native way — using tools like Claude Code and Claude Cowork to build lightweight tooling, accelerate experimentation, automate recurring workflows and analysis, and sharpen how the whole PLG function operates.

 - Influence across the business — representing the PLG stream in planning and growth reviews, aligning in-product and off-product growth motions, and helping shape the future direction of trade acquisition at Checkatrade.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience in Growth, Product, Operations, or Growth Marketing — with a track record of owning funnels, growth metrics, or revenue-impacting initiatives and making them move.

 - Strong analytical capability — comfortable with SQL (or equivalent), experiment design, statistical significance, and funnel modelling, and able to turn data into clear decisions.

 - A structured approach to experimentation — you’ve designed and run A/B tests, multivariate tests, or holdouts and know how to draw the right conclusions from them.

 - Hands-on experience with AI tools in your day-to-day work — you can walk through concrete examples of using Claude Code to build tooling or run analysis, and Claude Cowork to automate recurring workflows and the gains they delivered.
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!

 

How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with our Director of Trade Growth Products

 - 2nd Stage interview and case study with a panel from our Trade Growth team

 

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: Operations Manager (Trade)
source_jobs.description:
As our next Operations Manager (Trade), you'll be the engine behind how this function performs — without managing a team of your own. This is a pure strategy and enablement role: you'll sit alongside the Head of Operations, working across all three teams as an internal operator and problem-solver. You'll own the analytical framework, design and improve the processes the teams run on, and make sure the right insight reaches the right people at the right time.



This isn't a support role. It's a builder role. You'll have genuine ownership across analytics, operational excellence, and cross-functional delivery — with a direct line of sight to MRR, bad debt, cash collection, and trade satisfaction outcomes.



Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Analytics and insight: you will build and own the performance reporting framework across Retention, Credit Control, and Trade Support. Surface the right metrics — churn signals, collection rates, bad debt exposure, resolution times, satisfaction scores — and translate them into decisions that actually land with the teams and with Finance.

 - Process design and improvement: you will map end-to-end workflows across in-house and BPO teams, identify friction and failure points, and design scalable improvements — working within legacy system constraints while pushing for meaningful change.

 - Operational enablement: you will act as the connective tissue between the Head of Operations and the teams on the ground. You'll develop the frameworks, playbooks, and tools that help Retention, Credit Control, and Trade Support perform consistently — without being their line manager.

 - Cross-functional coordination: you will act as the connective layer between Trade Lifecycle, Finance, Product, CRM, and Engineering — making sure operational priorities are grounded in commercial outcomes and aligned to what those teams are building.

 - Execution and accountability: you will own the cadence of reviews, check-ins, and reporting cycles that keep the function honest and on track. Build the rhythms of execution — not just the strategy — so momentum is maintained week on week across all three teams.

 - Data infrastructure and tooling: you will identify gaps in reporting tooling, work with Engineering and CRM to close them, and build self-serve analytics capability so teams aren't dependent on ad-hoc data pulls to make decisions.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - A breadth of experience in a strategy, operations, or analytical role — ideally within a high-growth or marketplace business. Consulting backgrounds are equally welcome.

 - Strong SQL or equivalent data skills — you can get to the data yourself, not just interpret what others give you.

 - Commercial fluency — you understand what MRR, churn, bad debt, and cash collection mean in practice, not just in theory.

 - Experience working across operational teams — you know how retention, collections, or customer support functions work on the ground, and you can design for that reality.

 - Experience with BPO or vendor management — you've worked alongside external partners before and know how to drive improvement without managing them directly.

 - Proficiency with BI tooling (e.g. Tableau) — able to build self-serve reporting and define metrics frameworks (ideally), not just consume them.

 - Confidence with use of AI tools - specifically Claude
   
   

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + commission

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

source_jobs.title: Senior BPO Operations Specialist - 12 months FTC
source_jobs.description:
As our next Senior BPO Operations Specialist, you'll play a key role in helping us build the UK's go-to home improvement marketplace. Joining us on a 12-month maternity cover contract, you'll be the operational bridge between Checkatrade and key partners, keeping forecasting, reporting and quality frameworks running exactly as they should. It's a role built on continuity — you'll take the reins on well-established processes and make sure nothing drops while you're at the helm.

Forget long lists, here's what you'll actually spend your time on:

 - Acting as the primary operational liaison for our BPO partners managing a seamless handover at the start and end of the contract and maintaining trust throughout

 - Running our established monthly forecasting and invoice reconciliation processes, digging into variance analysis to catch and resolve unexpected cost changes fast

 - Keeping our BPO Quality Assurance frameworks and Tableau dashboards ticking over, running QA calibrations and live call listening to monitor compliance and interaction quality

 - Building and managing Statements of Work with Procurement to formalise vendor pilots and projects

 - Owning and delivering BPO performance presentations in Weekly Business Reviews, bringing data-backed rationale to an executive audience

 - Supporting the rollout of new strategic initiatives end-to-end, defining KPIs and spotting trends that drive retention and operational excellence

It's not about ticking every box. It's about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You'll do well here if you bring:

 - Experience managing outsourced BPO partnerships (or eagerness to learn), including SOW creation and navigating vendor transitions

 - Strong financial acumen — comfortable with cost forecasting, invoice reconciliation, variance analysis and scenario modelling

 - Confidence working with data visualisation tools like Tableau, and a knack for turning manual processes into something scalable

 - Excellent stakeholder communication, with experience (or eagerness to learn) presenting operational data at WBR/MBR/QBR level and navigating sensitive conversations

We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here's just a taste:

 - Competitive salary

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with the Hiring Manager

 - Final stage interview with a senior stakeholder panel

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we'd love to hear from you, even if you don't tick every box.

Want to join our Talent Community and be one of the first to know about our vacancies? Register your interest on our Registration Form https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/checkatrade/form/register-interest

At the moment, this role isn’t open to visa sponsorship applications.

About us

We’re Checkatrade – the UK’s leading platform for finding a tradesperson. With more than 50,000 trades listed and over 6 million reviews, we help homeowners get jobs done right and help tradespeople grow their business.

We’re growing fast, with big plans to become the go-to brand for home improvements. If you care about great work, take pride in the details, and want to make an impact, you’ll fit right in.

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Lead, SME
source_jobs.description:
As our next Growth Marketing Lead, SME Trade Acquisition, you'll own the engine that brings the right tradespeople into Checkatrade. This isn't a campaign management role — it's an end-to-end acquisition leadership position where you'll shape strategy, drive performance and help build the commercial foundation for the next phase of Checkatrade's growth. As we evolve from a lead-gen subscription business into a wider platform for UK trades, your work will directly determine how many of the right SME trades join us, at what cost, and with what quality.


Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Own SME acquisition across paid search, paid social, retargeting and database activation, making sure targeting, messaging, creative and conversion journeys are pulling in the same direction.

 - Build a sharp view of acquisition performance across CAC, lead quality, sign-ups, conversion and sales handoff, then use those signals to sharpen spend allocation and improve commercial outcomes.

 - Run a focused test-and-learn programme across ads, landing pages, journeys, creative and sales handoff, shipping tests fast, reading the results and driving the next round without waiting for perfect.

 - Work with Product Marketing and Product to sharpen the SME proposition and translate trade insight, channel data and Sales feedback into clear briefs and test plans.

 - Partner with Sales, Lifecycle and CRM to make sure acquisition activity feeds strong demand into the subscription business while building the entry points and nudges that support product-led growth over time.

 - Use AI actively across analysis, creative production, insight and workflow, helping the team move faster from insight to message to asset to test


It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - Previous B2B marketplace, subscription, SaaS or product-led growth, with a track record of improving conversion, activation and sales outcomes, not just running campaigns.

 - Strong commercial instincts: you think in CAC, LTV, cohorts and payback, and you use those lenses to shape channel mix, targeting and investment decisions.

 - Experience running test-and-learn programmes end to end, from hypothesis to result to decision, and comfort working across paid channels, landing pages, product entry points and sales journeys together.

 - Confidence using AI tools day to day, including for analysis, creative development, research, testing and workflow improvement, and comfort with tools like GA4, Looker, Amplitude or similar.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!


How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with one of our Talent Acquisition Partners

 - 1st stage interview with Senior Trade Growth Analyst and Senior Trade Marketing Designer

 - 2nd Stage interview with hiring Manager

 - Final stage interview includes a case study presentation with hiring manager

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box

source_jobs.title: VP Product
source_jobs.description:
At Checkatrade, our north star is Powering Great Work — transforming the landscape for trades, consumers, and the next generation of skilled workers. As our next VP Product, you’ll have the mandate to shape what that mission looks like in practice. This is a rare chance to own product strategy and execution at one of the UK’s most recognised home services brands — reporting directly to the CEO, working at the intersection of consumer experience, trade-side tooling, marketplace dynamics, and AI. The foundations are strong. The opportunity ahead is enormous.

Forget long lists, here’s what you’ll actually spend your time on:

 - Setting and owning the multi-year product vision and roadmap — translating Checkatrade’s growth ambitions into a clear, prioritised strategy that the whole business can get behind.

 - Leading, coaching and growing all Product Managers across the business — building a culture of ownership, curiosity, and pace, and creating the conditions for PMs to do their best work.

 - Driving rigorous prioritisation and planning — balancing short-term commercial impact with long-term platform investment, and keeping the organisation moving at pace.

 - Leading the integration of AI and machine learning across the product — from search and matching to personalisation and automation — identifying where these tools can genuinely unlock step-change improvements in experience and efficiency.

 - Partnering closely with Marketing, Engineering, Design, Data, and Commercial teams to make sure product decisions are grounded in real user needs and real business outcomes.

 - Representing the product function in board and investor conversations — communicating roadmap, progress, and trade-offs with clarity and confidence.



It’s not about ticking every box. It’s about turning up curious, ready to crack on. You’ll do well here if you bring:

 - A solid background in senior product leadership — you’ve owned product direction end-to-end and built high-performing PM teams from the ground up.

 - Proven experience in a two-sided marketplace or platform business — you understand supply and demand dynamics, network effects, and what it actually takes to grow a marketplace.

 - Demonstrable experience building and shipping AI-powered product features — from concept through to production, with a clear view on where the technology adds genuine value versus noise.

 - Strong commercial acumen and the ability to connect product decisions to revenue, retention, and growth — combined with exceptional communication skills that work equally well with a cross-functional team or a board room.



We believe great work deserves great rewards. Here’s just a taste:

 - Competitive salary + annual bonus

 - Employee Share Programme

 - Health, well-being and learning support

 - Gym membership, Smart Tech Scheme and Cycle Scheme

 - Birthday/Special day leave

 - Buy & sell holiday scheme

 - 1 week’s paid charity leave and much more!



How we’ll get to know each other:

 - Screening call with Laura Scott, Talent Acquisition

 - 30-minute interview with Mathieu Proust, Chief Operating Officer

 - Final stage interview with Jambu Palaniappan (CEO), Ashley Bartley (Chief People Officer), Mathieu Proust (Chief Operating Officer), and Gorden Pretorius (Chief Technology Officer)



Need sponsorship to work in the UK? We can support Skilled Worker visa applications for this role.

If this sounds like the place where you can do your best work, we’d love to hear from you, even if you don’t tick every box.
